key_insight: MA-ProofBench is the first formal theorem-proving benchmark focused on mathematical analysis, showing that current LLMs still struggle heavily with deeper formal reasoning in this domain.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark gives researchers a harder, more realistic testbed for measuring and improving theorem-proving systems beyond algebraic tasks.
method: Built 200 formalized theorems across 6 core topics and 27 subcategories in mathematical analysis.
Split problems into two tiers: 100 undergraduate-level and 100 Ph.D.-qualifier-level.
Used a human-led, LLM-assisted formalization pipeline with independent expert review.
evidence_signal: In evaluation, the best model (GPT-5.5) reached only 16% Pass@8 on Level I and 5% on Level II, with most models near 0% on Level II.
watch_item: Results may be influenced by Mathlib hallucinations and incomplete proofs, so benchmark performance does not fully reflect mathematical competence alone.
paper_title: MA-ProofBench: A Two-Tiered Evaluation of LLMs for Theorem Proving in Mathematical Analysis
